events.text: Betty Scott, 41-year-old Other female, Former smoker (quit 5 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Malignant melanoma of the Left upper back. Clinical staging: T3bN2aM1d, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C43.9. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent primary resection: Wide local excision with 2 cm margins and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Estimated blood loss 200 mL. Length of stay 5 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy: Dabrafenib 150 mg BID + Trametinib 2 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging on first-line BRAF/MEK-directed therapy.
Completed brain metastases treatment: Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) 20 Gy single fraction to 2 brain metastases. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated alternative first-line immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after alternative first-line immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after alternative first-line immunotherapy.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Date of Study: 2023-12-14

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.5 cm in the right lower lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Lytic lesion in L2 vertebral body measuring 1.5 cm. Suspicious for metastasis.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Specimen ID: S2023-22069
Date of Procedure: 2023-12-20
Date of Report: 2023-12-25

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Betty Scott - Left upper back" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 1.8 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Date: 2023-12-24
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Scott is a 41-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed malignant melanoma of the left upper back, IV. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation, lung and brain metastases. Pathology confirmed malignant melanoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports palpable mass noted by patient.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obesity
- hypertension
- hyperlipidemia

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: unintentional weight loss, fatigue, mild dyspnea on exertion, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 125/86, HR 68, RR 15, Temp 98.0F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 92.8 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.6, Hgb 12.2, Plt 179, Cr 1.18

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
41-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. initial oncology consultation.

1. Malignant melanoma of the left upper back, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Date of Report: 2024-01-01
Specimen: Left upper back (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Malignant melanoma
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 39.2 mutations/Mb (High)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRAF p.V600E (c.1799T>A) - Missense (Exon 15) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  NF1 p.T1556M - VUS
  ARID2 p.Q1334L - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRAF V600E: FDA-approved BRAF/MEK inhibitors include dabrafenib/trametinib, encorafenib/binimetinib, vemurafenib/cobimetinib.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 553x coverage.
Tumor content: 43%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Specimen ID: S2024-56054
Date of Procedure: 2024-01-20
Date of Report: 2024-01-22

SPECIMEN: Left upper back, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Malignant melanoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.2 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
S-100: Positive
HMB-45: Positive
Melan-A: Positive
SOX10: Positive
Ki-67: 30%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Betty Scott - Left upper back" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 2.2 x 1.5 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Date: 2024-01-25
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Scott is a 41-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Appetite is decreased but maintaining weight.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild dyspnea on exertion, mild peripheral edema. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 142/71, HR 65, RR 15, Temp 97.5F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 82.6 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.7, Hgb 12.3, Plt 269, Cr 0.95

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
41-year-old female with IV malignant melanoma of the left upper back. postoperative follow-up.

1. Malignant melanoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Monitoring for treatment-related toxicity
2. Symptom management
   - Fatigue management discussed
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Richard Okafor, MD
Medical Oncology

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Date of Study: 2024-12-29

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
BRAIN: 1 enhancing lesion identified: right temporal lobe (0.7 cm). Surrounding vasogenic edema. No midline shift.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

BRAIN MRI WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Betty Scott

Date of Study: 2025-08-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:

IMPRESSION:

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Betty Scott
MRN: patient_1d08aea93ccc
Date of Study: 2025-10-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: Malignant melanoma of the left upper back. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.6 cm in the right lower lobe. Stable in size.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Brain metastases as described. Recommend neurosurgical consultation.
2. Pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology
